folio.label: 156r
folio.l: ¢ Lady thyn bountee , thyn magnificence
Thy vertu , and thyn gret humylite
There may no tonge expresse , in no science
For som tyme lady , or men preyen to the
Thow gost biforn~ , of thyn benygnytee
And getest vs the ligút , of thyn preyere
To gyden vs , vn to thy sone so deere
¢ My konnyngö is so weyke , o blisful Quene
For to declare , thyn gret worthynesse
That I ne may , the weigút nat Sustene
But as a child , of twelmonetú old or lasse
That can vnethe , any word expässe
Rigút so fare I , & therfore I ©ow preye
Gydetú my songö , that I shal of ©ow seye
¢ Here bigynnetú the Prioresse , hire Tale
There was in Assye , in a gret Citee
A monges céstene folk , a Iewerie
Sustened by a lord , of that contree
For foul vsure , and lucre of vilenye
Hateful to crist , and to his compaignye
And thurgú this strete , men mygút ride or wende
For it was free , annd open at either ende
¢ A litel scole , of cristene folk there stode
Doun atte ferthere ende , in whicú there were
Children an hep~ , I comen of céstene blode
That lerned in that scole , ©er by ©ere
Swicú manere docténe , as men vsed there
This is to seyn , to syngen and to rede
As smale children doon , in here childhede
Amonges theise children , was a wydewes sone
A litel clergeoun , seuene ©er of age
That day by day , to scole was his wone
And eke also , where as he sey the ymage
Of cristes moder , had he in vsage
As hi~ was taugút , to knele a doun and seye
His Aue marie , as he gotú by the weye
¢ Thus hatú this wydewe , hire litel sone I taugút
Oure blisseful lady , cristes moder deere
To worshipe ay , and forgat it nougút
For sely Child , wil alwey sone lere
But ay whan I remembre , on this matere
Seynt Nicholas stant euäe , in my päsence
